title: Meta' Secret Plan To Kill The Smartphone — With Alex Himel
summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses Meta's strategy to position AI-powered smart glasses as the next major computing platform to replace smartphones. The conversation covers potential use cases including AI assistants for daily tasks and real-world context understanding, while addressing competition from OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and privacy concerns around facial recognition.
writeup: Alex Kantrowitz interviews Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, on the case for smart glasses as the next major computing platform for AI assistants. The discussion covers concrete use cases (meetings, reminders, and fitness tracking), the role of on-device AI, and competitive dynamics with OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon. It also addresses privacy concerns around facial recognition in wearable devices.
